# Conversation History

Initial date: YYYY-MM-DD
Project: [project name]
Scope: [folder, repository, or module]

## User Request

The user asked to:

- [main objective]
- [explicit constraints]
- [acceptance criteria]
- [relevant files, folders, or systems]

## Project Context

- Repository/folder: [path]
- Applied protocol: [Simplicity 1, 2, or 3]
- Current objective: [objective summary]
- Initial state verified: [what existed before the intervention]
- Documentation read: [README, TASKS, docs, ADRs, etc.]

## User Decisions and Preferences

- YYYY-MM-DD - [decision/preference] - [practical impact]
- YYYY-MM-DD - [decision/preference] - [practical impact]

## Actions Completed So Far

- YYYY-MM-DD - [action performed]
  - Files changed/created: [short list]
  - Relevant commands: [commands, if needed]
  - Result: [verified result]

## Current Verified State

- Last verification: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM
- Tests/commands executed: [commands and result]
- Active processes/services: [state]
- Generated artifacts: [files or outputs]
- Pending items: [unfinished items]
- Blockers: [real blockers, if any]

## Problems, Errors, and Fixes

- [problem or error]
  - Symptom: [how it appeared]
  - Probable/confirmed cause: [cause]
  - Fix applied: [fix]
  - Validation: [how it was validated]

## Next Steps

- [ ] [next concrete action]
- [ ] [pending validation]
- [ ] [decision needed from the user]

## Update Before Ending the Session

Date/time: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

- Summary of what changed in this session: [summary]
- What remains in progress: [state]
- What should be resumed first: [next action]
- Risks or cautions for the next session: [notes]

## Hygiene and Limits

- Do not record secrets, passwords, tokens, private keys, or unnecessary sensitive data.
- Record only context useful for resuming the project safely.
- Keep the text objective, summarized, and updated when there are relevant decisions, state changes, fixed errors, or new pending items.
- When there are reusable lessons for other projects, evaluate with the user whether they should also go into `global-history-chat.md`.
